title: SICP 5.4 explicit-control evaluator
goal: Run the SICP 5.4 explicit-control evaluator program on top of the existing register-machine simulator and make it a passing JIT/AOT SICP gate probe.
next_actions: Add tests/sicp/ch5_explicit_control_evaluator.esk reusing the existing register-machine simulator patterns.
Encode the explicit-control evaluator controller with registers, stack operations, primitive application, environment operations, and eval/apply dispatch.
Run representative evaluator programs: self-evaluating expressions, primitive calls, definitions, conditionals, lambda application, and recursion.
Keep the program bounded and sourceful when a simulator operation is missing.
Avoid depending on native shortcuts that skip the register-machine evaluator path.
acceptance: tests/sicp/ch5_explicit_control_evaluator.esk exists and self-checks the SICP 5.4 evaluator.
The probe passes under AOT compile/run.
ICC sicp-completeness observes sicp_ch5_explicit_control_evaluator_r and sicp_ch5_explicit_control_evaluator_aot as PASS.
status_note: Implemented on PR #103 as tests/sicp/ch5_explicit_control_evaluator.esk. Focused JIT and AOT checks pass for evaluator registers, save/restore stack, eval/apply dispatch, definitions, mutation, conditionals, closures, recursion, and stack-balance statistics. Awaiting full SICP smoke/ICC gate before marking done.
ux_payoff: Proves Eshkol can host SICP's evaluator at the register-machine level, not only as direct high-level interpreter code.
